notes: Pickleheads lists 5 locations totaling 11 courts for Saratoga (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/saratoga), but 3 of those 5 listings are not genuine public venues. 'McCarty Park' and 'McCarty Mansion' are the exact same address (18444 Swarthmore Dr) -- a 3bd/2ba single-family residence built 1955 per property records -- listed twice under two different names, each with 1 court and marked 'membership required'/'private' even on Pickleheads itself. 'Versailles' (19653 Versailles Way) is a separate private residence that sold for $4.25M in 2019 with a backyard tennis/pickleball court, also marked private/membership. Both addresses fit the pattern of privately-owned home courts monetized via hourly/membership rental platforms (Swimply independently lists 'Top 10 Pickleball Courts for Rent in Saratoga, CA'), not public venues. Per this site's public-venue standard, these 3 listings (accounting for 3 of Pickleheads' 11 stated courts) are excluded from 'venues' below and are not real candidates for this directory. The 2 remaining genuine Pickleheads-listed public venues -- El Quito Park (2 courts) and West Valley College (6 courts) -- account for 8 of the stated 11 courts and are included. Cross-checked against the official City of Saratoga parks list (saratoga.ca.us/222/Park-Information, 15 parks total): El Quito Park is confirmed as the only city park with pickleball; Beauchamps Park has a non-reservable tennis court but no source confirms pickleball lines/nets there, so it was left out rather than assumed; Congress Springs Park has no court facilities at all (baseball/soccer fields only). Two additional legitimate venues that don't appear on Pickleheads at all were found via other sources and added: Brookside Club of Saratoga (private membership tennis/swim club, 2 temporary/converted pickleball courts) and Saratoga Country Club (private country club, 6 shared tennis/pickleball courts). No dedicated public indoor pickleball facility was found anywhere in Saratoga -- all 4 included venues are outdoor. Note for future researchers: a promising-looking 'Group Is Building A Facility For Pickleball Courts' headline turned out to be from saratoga.com's Business Journal, which covers Saratoga Springs, NY, not the City of Saratoga, CA -- a name collision in the same spirit as the 'Oakland, NJ' mismatch flagged in this repo's research-prompts doc. Ignored as irrelevant.

venues.name: El Quito Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated pickleball courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.price: $24/hour court reservation via LGS Recreation (one secondary source cited $18/hour -- see notes)
venues.hours: Park open daily 6am-9pm; the 2 pickleball courts are reservable via LGS Recreation -- unreserved walk-up availability is not confirmed by any source
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay foothill town -- a touch cooler and greener than the valley floor thanks to the adjacent Santa Cruz Mountains foothills, dry summers.
venues.notes: The City of Saratoga's own park page and Pickleheads agree: 2 outdoor hard pickleball courts, dedicated permanent lines/nets, reservable through LGS Recreation (the joint-powers recreation authority serving Saratoga and Los Gatos) at $24/hour -- corroborated by two independent search passes both citing the LGS booking system. One secondary aggregator-synthesized result cited an $18/hour rate instead; $24/hour was used here since it's the figure Pickleheads itself states and it was independently corroborated, but a human should re-verify current pricing directly with LGS Recreation (408-256-7145 / facilityrentals@lgsrecreation.org) since rental rates change. Whether walk-up play is possible when the courts aren't reserved is not explicitly confirmed by any source, so 'reservable' was set to Reservable rather than Mixed to avoid assuming. Lights not confirmed either way by any source checked, despite the park otherwise being well-documented. An address discrepancy was resolved during research: one early web-search synthesis said '19855 Paseo Presada,' but the official city page, Yelp, and a direct Google Maps lookup all independently agree on 12855 Paseo Presada -- the corroborated address was used.

venues.name: West Valley College
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, dedicated pickleball courts with permanent lines and nets, separate from the college's 14-16 tennis courts
venues.hours: Not consistently specified across sources -- described as free/first-come-first-served by Pickleheads and most aggregators, but at least one source describes hours tied to the class/team schedule (see notes)
venues.skill: Community/player-review aggregators describe an organized paddle-rotation system with skill level typically 3.5+
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay foothill town -- a touch cooler and greener than the valley floor thanks to the adjacent Santa Cruz Mountains foothills, dry summers.
venues.notes: Consistently described across many independent sources (Pickleheads, West Valley College's own facility-rentals and athletics pages, and half a dozen pickleball-specific aggregators) as 6 dedicated outdoor pickleball courts, separate from the college's 14-16 tennis courts, free and lit, with a $3 parking fee -- high confidence on the core facts. Address used (19281 San Marcos Rd) is what both Pickleheads and a direct Google Maps lookup use for the specific 'Pickleball courts | West Valley College' pin, distinct from the college's general mailing address (14000 Fruitvale Ave) -- the athletic complex sits on a different part of the 143-acre campus, reached via San Marcos Rd / S College Cir. Hours are genuinely unclear and conflict across sources: some describe unrestricted free first-come-first-served access, one aggregator states Mon-Thu 9am-5pm only (closed Fri-Sun), and the college's own facility-rentals page describes courts 1-2 (the tennis courts, possibly extending to pickleball) as blocked for team use Mon-Fri 4:30pm-7:30pm and weekends 11am-3pm, implying public play happens outside those windows. Rather than pick one conflicting source, 'hours' is left as a summary -- a human should call West Valley College Athletics (408-741-2017 / 408-867-2200) to confirm the current public-play schedule.

venues.name: Brookside Club of Saratoga
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court; 2 courts temporarily striped/converted for pickleball (the club also has 7 separate dedicated tennis-only courts)
venues.netType: Portable nets on temporary/converted tennis courts
venues.price: Membership required (private club); pickleball clinics $45/month commitment or $50 drop-in per session
venues.hours: Club open daily 8am-8pm (member/guest access; may vary on holidays); pickleball clinics Mon & Thu 10-11am (beginner), Fri 10:30-11:30am (intermediate, 2.5-3.5 rated)
venues.skill: Beginner and Intermediate (2.5-3.5) adult clinics offered; also runs a day league with divisions/teams for various levels
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay foothill town -- a touch cooler and greener than the valley floor thanks to the adjacent Santa Cruz Mountains foothills, dry summers.
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads at all -- found via tennispickleballcalifornia.com and the club's own pickleball-lessons page (brooksidetennistraining.com), which independently agree on 2 pickleball courts. A third source (Leagues For Pickleball's TopDog club page) lists '9' total courts for the club, which is consistent with (not contradicting) 7 dedicated tennis courts + 2 temporary/converted pickleball courts described elsewhere. Google Maps confirms the club's existence and address as a 'Private club providing members with tennis courts & a swimming pool, plus lessons for kids & adults,' though that short description doesn't itself name pickleball. Courts are explicitly described elsewhere as 'temporary' (tennis courts converted with portable nets/lines), not dedicated pickleball-only courts -- reflected in surface/netType. Guests may attend accompanied by a member per one source; the reservation mechanics for casual (non-lesson) member play aren't described by any source, so 'reservable' is marked Mixed rather than guessed precisely. No court lighting, stated explicitly by tennispickleballcalifornia.com.

venues.name: Saratoga Country Club
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court, all 6 lighted courts dual-lined for both tennis and pickleball (shared/dual-use, not pickleball-dedicated)
venues.price: Membership required (private country club; initiation fee + dues not publicly listed)
venues.hours: Not specified (club hours not published; Google Maps lists the property opening 7am)
venues.skill: Junior and adult clinics for various skill levels; private instruction available with a dedicated pickleball pro (Albert Lee) alongside the tennis pro staff
venues.weather: Warm, sunny South Bay foothill town -- a touch cooler and greener than the valley floor thanks to the adjacent Santa Cruz Mountains foothills, dry summers.
venues.notes: Not listed on Pickleheads. Confirmed on the club's own site (saratogacc.com/Racquet: 'six courts... play private tennis or pickleball,' 'fully lighted') and independently repeated across several golf/country-club directories (Go Private Golf, clubmembership.info) and a general pickleball-court aggregator (teachme.to). Caveat: most of the corroborating directory listings appear to derive from the club's own marketing copy rather than an independent player report -- no Yelp review or forum post specifically mentioning pickleball play there turned up in this pass, so treat the pickleball claim as club-stated rather than player-verified. All 6 courts are shared/dual-lined for tennis and pickleball, not pickleball-dedicated; net type for pickleball play (portable vs. adjusted permanent) isn't specified anywhere. Exclusive private country club -- membership (initiation + dues) required, pricing not published.
